text:
The start of2024 Olympicshave been marred by a series of burglaries, with two separate camps targeted by thieves. Argentina's football team has had a particularly difficult start to the Games, suffering not only a controversial loss to Morocco in their opening match but also a break-in at their Lyon base.

Manager Javier Mascherano, formerly ofBarcelonaandLiverpool, revealed on Thursday that jewellery was stolen from their accommodation the day before they wereforced off the pitchdue to crowd trouble during their first group match on Wednesday. In a surprising turn of events, Argentina lost 2-1 to Morocco, despite believing they had scored a dramatic equaliser in the 15th minute of injury time at the end of the second half.

However, as the team celebrated, projectiles and a flare were thrown onto the pitch, landing near the Argentine bench. The match was subsequently suspended when several Moroccan fans invaded the pitch, prompting intervention from riot police.

Spectators were asked to leave the Saint Etienne stadium, and the players had to endure a nearly two-hour wait for the match to resume without any fans present. During this delay, it was announced that Argentina's equaliser had beendisallowed following a VAR review. This meant that when play resumed, Morocco were leading 2-1, and ended up holding on to their lead to win the game, marking Argentina's second setback in just two days.

"While we were training, someone robbed us," Mascherano, 40, raged post-match. "We are talking about the Olympic Games. Thiago Almada's watch, rings, everything... were missing. During a training session at the Olympic Games. We didn't want to say anything after training, but well..

"They ask us for credentials for everything, but then these things happen. Today they invaded the pitch seven times and then they threw firecrackers at us."



French media outletBFMTVreported that jewellery worth around £42,000 was stolen, citing a judicial source. Argentina has since lodged an official complaint with FIFA's Disciplinary Committee over the pitch invasion amid concerns about the safety of their players. Additionally, authorities have initiated an investigation into the theft of Almada's possessions.

As it happens, the 23-year-old footballer isn't the only Olympian to fall victim to theft this week. The Australian BMX squad revealed their van had been broken into, resulting in a smashed window and the loss of various items from within.

Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

"During the night our van was robbed, luckily my bikes weren't in there," Olympian Logan Martin revealed on social media. "But my bike bag was with some belongings. What a crazy way to start the trip."

The incident happened in Brussels, Belgium, where the Australian team had been training prior to heading to Paris for Friday's opening ceremony. Thankfully, Martin later confirmed that the stolen items, including bike gear and a wallet, were recovered.

title: Olympic camps rocked as thieves nick jewellery worth £42,000 and a van | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Brent
description: The opening days of the 2024 Olympics have been marred by a number of unsavoury incidents, with two separate athlete camps targeted by thieves in recent days.
